title: Isoglossa subcordata
taxonomy: Isoglossa subcordata is classified within the family Acanthaceae, a large family of flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or acanthus family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Isoglossa subcordata is specifically placed within the subfamily Acanthoideae. The genus Isoglossa is a part of this subfamily and is characterized by its unique characteristics and adaptations. Isoglossa subcordata is a species within this genus, and its classification is based on its morphological and anatomical features. The classification of Isoglossa subcordata is a part of the ongoing process of botanical research and classification.

The plant is also believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Isoglossa subcordata is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion. parts: Isoglossa subcordata, also known as the lesser yam or lesser taro, is a plant species native to various regions in Asia and the Pacific Islands. The medicinal parts of this plant are its leaves, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their potential health benefits. The leaves of Isoglossa subcordata are said to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making them useful in treating various health conditions.
